title: Local Agent
content:
# Adding a Local AI Agent to This Blog

I added a conversational AI assistant to the blog — the chat bubble in the bottom-right corner of every page. It runs entirely in the browser with no backend and no API cost, using [WebLLM](https://github.com/mlc-ai/web-llm) to run a quantised Llama 3.1 model locally via WebGPU.

## Model

I used **Hermes-3-Llama-3.1-8B-q4f16_1-MLC** — a Nous Research fine-tune of Meta's Llama 3.1 8B, quantised to 4-bit weights. It was tuned for function-calling, which drives the tool loop. The weights are about 2 GB, downloaded once and cached in the browser. WebGPU is required, so it works in Chrome and Edge on GPU-enabled devices.

## Architecture

The agent is a React component (`BlogAgent.tsx`) in the Next.js layout, so it appears on every page. Tool definitions and helper functions live in `agent-tools.ts`. Post metadata is pre-built at deploy time into `agent-data.json`, which the component fetches when the panel opens.

```typescript
const { CreateMLCEngine } = await import('@mlc-ai/web-llm');
const engine = await CreateMLCEngine(
  'Hermes-3-Llama-3.1-8B-q4f16_1-MLC',
  { initProgressCallback: ({ progress, text }) => setLoadState(...) },
  { context_window_size: 8192 },
);
```

The agent has six tools:

| Tool | What it does |
|------|-------------|
| `search_posts` | Keyword search across titles, descriptions, and tags |
| `get_posts_by_category` | All posts in a named category |
| `list_categories` | All categories ranked by post count |
| `get_post_content` | Full Markdown content of a specific post |
| `navigate_to_post` | Push the browser to a post via the Next.js router |
| `web_search` | Live web search via Jina AI (optional) |

## Two-Phase Design

Hermes-3 sometimes returns `null` text content when tools are present, even on a `stop` finish. I worked around this with a two-phase loop: phase 1 runs with tools enabled and collects results; phase 2 makes a clean call with no tools, embedding the collected results as plain text in the user message. Without tools present the model always returns text.

## Bugs Fixed After Launch

**Context window overflow.** Asking "Any Java related posts?" caused the model to call `search_posts` twice then call `get_post_content` on eight posts at once, producing ~50,000 characters of results against an 8,192-token context window. I fixed this with three guards: deduplication (same tool + args skipped within a turn), a cap of two `get_post_content` calls per turn, and a 3,500-character cap on combined tool results before they're embedded in the phase-2 prompt.

```typescript
const callKey = `${tc.function.name}:${JSON.stringify(args)}`;
if (calledThisTurn.has(callKey)) {
  apiMsgs.push({ role: 'tool', tool_call_id: tc.id, content: '(duplicate call skipped)' });
  continue;
}
calledThisTurn.add(callKey);
``` I also added a rule steering the model to use `get_posts_by_category` for category questions — the text search was matching "java" inside "JavaScript" post descriptions, causing bad results and retries.

**Wrong tool for "summarise this post".** On a post page, asking "summarise this post" triggered `get_posts_by_category` instead of `get_post_content`. The model was reading the post's category metadata and following the category-browsing rule ahead of the post-page rule. I moved the post-page rule to first position in the context hint with an explicit highest-priority label and hard-prohibited any other tool for that case. I also made the nudge message context-aware — on a post page it now names the exact slug to fetch, rather than suggesting category browsing.

```typescript
const nudge = isOnPostPage && currentSlug
  ? `Call get_post_content with slug "${currentSlug}" to read this post's content.`
  : 'Call search_posts or get_posts_by_category to find the information before answering.';
```

- [MLC AI — Machine Learning Compilation](https://mlc.ai)
- [Hermes-3-Llama-3.1-8B on Hugging Face](https://huggingface.co/NousResearch/Hermes-3-Llama-3.1-8B)
